event: contacted for transfer to Stanford Hospital
6.5 cm Stanford Type A aortic dissection
subarachnoid hemorrhage in right parietal lobe
mobile mass in left ventricular outflow tract
disseminated intravascular coagulation secondary to sepsis
received 4 U packed red blood cells
received 3 vials activated prothrombin complex concentrate
replacement of aortic root with bioprosthetic aortic valve
dissection flap extending into aneurysmal root
replacement of noncoronary cusp by vegetation
no evidence of malperfusion of abdominal viscera
right maxillary bone graft several months prior
punctate infarcts with microhemorrhages in brain
ocular ischemic syndrome secondary to Type A dissection
probable abscess at prior right maxillary bone graft
open tract between right maxillary sinus and oral cavity
taken to operating room for debridement
spontaneous movement of all extremities on postoperative day 1
transferred to ward on postoperative day 8
discharged to rehabilitation facility on postoperative day 30
returned to emergency department with pleuritic chest pain
readmitted for contrast extravasation in perigraft fluid collection
